message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "rust" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: std::fs::write("outrunner.yml") writes to whatever cwd happens to be and silently clobbers existing config
DETAIL: src/config.rs line 248: `std::fs::write("outrunner.yml", config)?`. Relative path means `gha-isolated init` run from $HOME, from /, or from a systemd unit (cwd=/) scatters config files unpredictably — and the very next feature on the roadmap is `install-service`, where a service's cwd is not the user's shell cwd. It also overwrites an existing outrunner.yml with no prompt, no backup, no --force. There is no `.context()` on the write, so a permission failure reports a bare OS error with no path context.
RECOMMENDATION: Add the `directories` crate and write to `ProjectDirs::from("", "", "gha-isolated").config_dir()/outrunner.yml` (Linux: ~/.config/gha-isolated/, macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/). Refuse to overwrite unless --force. Wrap IO in `.with_context(|| format!("writing {}", path.display()))`. Store the resolved path so start/stop/status read the same file the init wrote.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.description: Find outrunner.yml references in design doc
Check if repo contains a Rust implementation of this design

content.content: [REDACTED for publication: verbatim content from a private repository (worldarchitect.ai self-hosted runner infrastructure) was returned by this tool call and has been removed. The reviewer's analysis of it is preserved in subsequent assistant messages.]

input.reasoning: Every factual element of the finding verifies against the doc: line 248 is exactly `std::fs::write("outrunner.yml", config)?;` with a cwd-relative path, no overwrite guard (Init takes no flags, fs::write truncates unconditionally), and no .context() (anyhow::Context is never imported despite anyhow being a dependency). The relative-path concern is not speculative: runner.rs line 258 reads the same relative "outrunner.yml", and the doc itself defines an InstallService subcommand (line 124) and lists systemd/launchd service installation as next-step item 2 (line 289) — systemd units default to cwd=/, so a service would miss config written to the operator's shell cwd. The recommendation is sound and consistent with the doc's own framing: Init is described as "Initialize configuration for this machine" (machine-scoped, not per-project docker-compose-style), making an XDG/ProjectDirs config location the correct contract; --force gating and .with_context() are standard, low-cost fixes. The "it's only a skeleton" objection does not refute severity — design review is precisely where a config-location contract spanning config.rs, runner.rs, and the planned service.rs should be fixed, and silent clobber of existing config is a data-loss-class defect. Major severity is proportionate. No implementation exists in /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner that supersedes or fixes the skeleton.
